req.title: Recurring transfer (negative variant)
req.statement: A premium-tier customer shall be able to schedule a recurring transfer up to $10,000 per occurrence; non-premium accounts capped at $2,500. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-055 defines two equivalence partitions for per-occurrence limits (standard ≤ $2,500; premium ≤ $10,000) and explicit error paths for exceeded limits, insufficient funds, and unsupported frequency. The test suite covers: (1) a positive EP case confirming a premium customer can schedule within their cap and receives a persisted schedule with a calculated next-run date; (2) a negative NEG case confirming a standard-tier customer is rejected with HTTP 422 when the requested amount exceeds $2,500; (3) a BVA boundary case at exactly $2,500 for a standard customer (upper boundary, must succeed); (4) a BVA boundary case at $2,500 + $0.01 for a standard customer (just-above boundary, must fail); (5) a NEG case for an unsupported frequency value; (6) a DT case combining insufficient-funds signal with a valid-tier/valid-limit request, expecting 422. This satisfies the minimum three-case requirement while providing broad heuristic coverage traceable to R-FIN-055.

tests.name: test_premium_recurring_transfer_within_limit_succeeds
preconditions: Amount $8,000 is within premium cap of $10,000
actions: POST /transfers/recurring with premium auth token, amount=8000, frequency=WEEKLY
expected: Response body contains next_run_date in ISO-8601 format
Response body reflects amount=8000 and frequency=WEEKLY
trace: R-FIN-055: schedule persisted; next run calculated
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime

def test_premium_recurring_transfer_within_limit_succeeds(<api_client>, <premium_auth_headers>, <assert_iso8601>):
    payload = {
        "amount": 8000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "WEEKLY",
        "destination_account": "<dest_account_id>"
    }
    resp = <api_client>.post("/transfers/recurring", json=payload, headers=<premium_auth_headers>)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "schedule_id" in body, "Missing schedule_id"
    assert "next_run_date" in body, "Missing next_run_date"
    <assert_iso8601>(body["next_run_date"])
    assert body["amount"] == 8000
    assert body["frequency"] == "WEEKLY"

tests.name: test_standard_recurring_transfer_exceeds_cap_returns_422
actions: POST /transfers/recurring with standard auth token, amount=5000, frequency=MONTHLY
expected: Response body error code indicates per-occurrence limit exceeded
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_standard_recurring_transfer_exceeds_cap_returns_422(<api_client>, <standard_auth_headers>):
    payload = {
        "amount": 5000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "MONTHLY",
        "destination_account": "<dest_account_id>"
    }
    resp = <api_client>.post("/transfers/recurring", json=payload, headers=<standard_auth_headers>)
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "detail" in body, "Missing error descriptor in response"
    error_text = str(body.get("error", body.get("detail", ""))).lower()
    assert "limit" in error_text or "exceed" in error_text, f"Unexpected error message: {error_text}"

tests.name: test_standard_recurring_transfer_at_exact_cap_boundary_succeeds
preconditions: Amount $2,500 is exactly at the standard-tier cap boundary
actions: POST /transfers/recurring with standard auth token, amount=2500, frequency=MONTHLY
trace: R-FIN-055: non-premium accounts capped at $2,500 (inclusive upper boundary)
R-FIN-055: schedule persisted; next run calculated
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_standard_recurring_transfer_at_exact_cap_boundary_succeeds(<api_client>, <standard_auth_headers>, <assert_iso8601>):
    payload = {
        "amount": 2500,
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "MONTHLY",
        "destination_account": "<dest_account_id>"
    }
    resp = <api_client>.post("/transfers/recurring", json=payload, headers=<standard_auth_headers>)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201 at boundary, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "schedule_id" in body
    assert "next_run_date" in body
    <assert_iso8601>(body["next_run_date"])

tests.name: test_standard_recurring_transfer_one_cent_above_cap_returns_422
preconditions: Amount $2,500.01 is one cent above the standard-tier cap boundary
actions: POST /transfers/recurring with standard auth token, amount=2500.01, frequency=MONTHLY
expected: Response body error indicates per-occurrence limit exceeded
trace: R-FIN-055: non-premium accounts capped at $2,500; $2,500.01 must be rejected
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_standard_recurring_transfer_one_cent_above_cap_returns_422(<api_client>, <standard_auth_headers>):
    payload = {
        "amount": 2500.01,
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "MONTHLY",
        "destination_account": "<dest_account_id>"
    }
    resp = <api_client>.post("/transfers/recurring", json=payload, headers=<standard_auth_headers>)
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422 just above boundary, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    error_text = str(body.get("error", body.get("detail", ""))).lower()
    assert "limit" in error_text or "exceed" in error_text

tests.name: test_unsupported_frequency_returns_422
preconditions: Frequency value 'HOURLY' is not in the supported set
actions: POST /transfers/recurring with premium auth token, amount=100, frequency=HOURLY
expected: Response body error indicates unsupported frequency
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_unsupported_frequency_returns_422(<api_client>, <premium_auth_headers>):
    payload = {
        "amount": 100,
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "HOURLY",
        "destination_account": "<dest_account_id>"
    }
    resp = <api_client>.post("/transfers/recurring", json=payload, headers=<premium_auth_headers>)
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422 for unsupported frequency, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    error_text = str(body.get("error", body.get("detail", ""))).lower()
    assert "frequency" in error_text or "unsupported" in error_text, f"Unexpected error: {error_text}"

tests.name: test_insufficient_funds_returns_422
preconditions: Account balance is $100 (insufficient for requested amount)
Amount $500 is within standard cap but exceeds available balance
actions: POST /transfers/recurring with standard auth token, amount=500, frequency=WEEKLY
Banking API performs balance pre-check before persisting schedule
expected: Response body error indicates insufficient funds
trace: R-FIN-055: negative path - insufficient funds must be tested
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_insufficient_funds_returns_422(<api_client>, <standard_low_balance_auth_headers>):
    # <standard_low_balance_auth_headers> authenticates a standard-tier account
    # seeded with $100 balance via <seed_account_balance> fixture
    payload = {
        "amount": 500,
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "WEEKLY",
        "destination_account": "<dest_account_id>"
    }
    resp = <api_client>.post("/transfers/recurring", json=payload, headers=<standard_low_balance_auth_headers>)
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422 for insufficient funds, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    error_text = str(body.get("error", body.get("detail", ""))).lower()
    assert "insufficient" in error_text or "funds" in error_text or "balance" in error_text, \
        f"Expected insufficient-funds error, got: {error_text}"

tests.name: test_premium_cap_state_transition_standard_to_premium_unlocks_higher_limit
preconditions: Customer account was previously STANDARD tier (cap $2,500)
Customer account has been upgraded to PREMIUM tier (cap $10,000)
actions: POST /transfers/recurring with upgraded-premium auth token, amount=9000, frequency=MONTHLY
expected: Schedule persisted with amount=9000 reflecting premium-tier entitlement
trace: R-FIN-055: tier state transition standard->premium; premium cap $10,000 applies
R-FIN-055: schedule persisted; next run calculated
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_premium_cap_state_transition_standard_to_premium_unlocks_higher_limit(
    <api_client>, <upgraded_to_premium_auth_headers>, <assert_iso8601>
):
    # <upgraded_to_premium_auth_headers> uses an account seeded as STANDARD
    # then upgraded via <upgrade_account_tier> fixture before this test runs
    payload = {
        "amount": 9000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "MONTHLY",
        "destination_account": "<dest_account_id>"
    }
    resp = <api_client>.post("/transfers/recurring", json=payload, headers=<upgraded_to_premium_auth_headers>)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201 after tier upgrade, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "schedule_id" in body
    assert body["amount"] == 9000
    <assert_iso8601>(body["next_run_date"])
